Tithonia is a flowering plant of the daisy family, native to Mexico and Central America, often used as an ornamental plant due to its vibrant flowers. The health benefits of Tithonia include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Tithonia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tithonia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
